commit.message:
Fix 15 prediction model bugs across pipeline, training, and infrastructure

Critical pipeline fixes (Phase 1):
- UNDER bets no longer use wrong over_odds for EV/Kelly calculation
- under_odds defaults changed from -110 to None to prevent zero-edge devigging
- Best bet edge thresholds now prop-specific instead of uniform 2.0
- PRA std_dev corrected from 8.5 to 7.9 (was compressing Z-scores 13%)
- Line validation rejects None/zero lines before edge calculation
- Removed redundant MIN_EDGE=999 entries; DISABLED_PROPS is single source of truth
- Sigma floor reduced from 50% to 35% allowing higher quantile confidence
- Predictions cache TTL increased from 30s to 300s

Training data fixes (Phase 2, require retraining):
- Removed hardcoded +3.0 home court advantage feature leakage
- Added 12-minute filter to exclude garbage time from props training
- Removed OT stat normalization (props settle on actuals, not normalized)
- Labeled 2023-24 evaluation as [IN-SAMPLE] since it was used in training

Infrastructure safety (Phase 3):
- Quantile calibration failure now blocks deployment and restores backups
- Incremental retraining regenerates OOF predictions before meta-learner update

Estimated combined ROI impact: +5-12%

working days, ignoring the role-based counting method (WEEKDAYS_IF_WORKING
for MARs, MONDAY_TO_SATURDAY for IADEs). This caused incorrect balance
deductions - e.g. 9 days deducted instead of 5 for a MAR Mon-Sun leave.

Backend: replaced raw calendar diff with getCountingConfigForLeave() +
calculateWorkingDays() including public holiday exclusion.
Frontend: replaced local duration calc with API call to
/api/leaves/calculate-working-days with leaveTypeCode + leaveUserId.

Fix second flash: only re-fit on width changes, not height changes

autoFitLyrics changes font size which changes container height, causing
ResizeObserver to fire again and trigger a second dim/calculate cycle.
Now only re-runs when container width changes (>= 10px), which covers
orientation changes and window resizes but ignores height-only changes
caused by the font-size adjustment itself.
